name: Rob's Focaccia with Eggplant, Tomatoes & Anchovies
recipeInstructions:
1. Let the bread dough rise in a heavy-weight bowl, covered with plastic wrap.
2. Overnight, it will be ready for dinner the following day.
3. Peel the eggplant and slice into about 1/4 inch thick circles. Place on a greased cookie sheet and bake at 350°F until browned and slightly soft (about 20 minutes).
4. Peel the tomatoes, dip in boiling water to separate skins, then cut into small thin rounds and drain.
5. Remove seeds from the tomatoes.
6. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
7. Take the risen dough and stretch it into a circle of about 12-13 inches. It may try to recede; let it rest for 5 minutes before proceeding.
8. Place the baked eggplant slices over the dough, then arrange tomato slices over the eggplant. Sprinkle chopped anchovies (if desired) and fresh basil leaves whole. Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle grated Parmesan or mozzarella on top.
9. Bake in the preheated oven for about 20 minutes, increasing the temperature to 450°F for the last 10 minutes.
10. Serve with plenty of red wine.
